global_rules: This successor pack begins after Gates 51–58 and exists to formalise lawful adaptation before any historical-data testing begins.
The binding cognition grammar order remains authoritative; no gate may reorder runtime stages unless a dedicated gate proves and documents the change.
One branch per gate; complete every planned leaf for the gate, pass targeted validation, merge to main, and only then open the next gate branch.
Historical replay is the research/discovery surface; live paper is the falsification/promotion surface for locked candidates and must not become in-place coefficient discovery.
Runtime never invents new coefficients or hidden policy in place; it may only apply approved invariant, baseline, and state-conditioned policy surfaces frozen by earlier gates.
Review does not imply change. “No change” is a valid governed review outcome.
The V6 pair is self-contained and controlling. Earlier successor drafts are archival provenance only and must not be treated as governing inputs.
Any gate that reveals ontology failure or a missing module must emit a research-reset note rather than smuggling redesign into the current gate.

leaves.title: Rebase normative doctrine for lawful adaptation and stable-doctrine / variable-posture language
leaves.scope: Update binding doctrine so the tranche starts from the right philosophy before any implementation gate lands.
implementation_tasks: Update the normative/planning doctrine to state that the repo preserves a stable cognition grammar and only varies operating posture through bounded approved state.
State explicitly that historical replay is the discovery surface and live paper is the falsification/promotion surface.
Record anti-language that would imply runtime self-invention or daily strategy reinvention.
validation_tasks: Check the revised doctrine against docs/01_NORMATIVE.md, docs/02_OPERATING_MODEL.md, docs/05_GUARDRAILS.md, and current gate history for wording conflicts.
leaves.pass_gate: Rebase normative doctrine for lawful adaptation and stable-doctrine / variable-posture language is explicit, bounded, and consistent with the governing cognitive-workflow tranche rules.
leaves.merge_gate: Gate 59 remains a bounded sequential implementation slice and does not silently absorb later gates.

historical_note: v6 preserves the corrected dependency order that puts adaptation doctrine, review law, and candidate governance ahead of context plumbing. Gate 59 makes the V6 pair self-contained and authoritative inside the repo. The attached `_v4_5` salvage pair is archived under docs/legacy for provenance; missing `v4` or `v5` drafts are not required to execute Gates 60–79.
